title: Write training_manifest.json with dataset hash, base model, hyperparams, runtime, exit code
completion_summary: src/apprentice_trainer/manifest_writer.py: build_manifest() returns a dict with schema_version (1), created_at (ISO-8601 UTC), dataset_dir, dataset_hash (read from <dataset_dir>/manifest.json's sha256 -- the bridge back to dataset-builder's reproducibility), base_model, hyperparameters (allow-listed via collect_hyperparameters so paths/--verbose/--check-only don't pollute the record), runtime_seconds, exit_code, plus optional `extra` for adapter_dir etc. write_manifest() does atomic write via tempfile + os.replace AND uses json.dumps(sort_keys=True, indent=2) so the byte stream is deterministic -- trainer-07's Ed25519 sig is reproducible. train.py: training is now wrapped in try/except; a crash sets exit_code=1 and still writes the manifest so the validator milestone has a record of what was attempted. The dataset_hash bridge means the validator can verify 'this trained model came from THIS exact JSONL split.' 10 new pytest cases (28 then, all pass on CPU): hash present/missing/malformed/no-sha-field, required-fields contract, JSON validity round-trip, deterministic-byte-output for identical input (signing prereq), atomic-write leaves no .tmp, hyperparameter allow-list filters out paths + flags, failure manifest records non-zero exit. Deliverables: trainer/src/apprentice_trainer/manifest_writer.py + tests/test_manifest_writer.py + integration into train.py.
acceptance_criteria: Manifest contains dataset_hash, base_model, hyperparameters, runtime_seconds, exit_code
